upcoming.subtitle: Inaugural in-person sprint — 50 builders, solo competition, co-hosted with inkbox.ai & Genesis Fund

venue.name: Back Bay (exact address after approval)

upcoming.description: First-ever Cursor Boston Hack-a-Sprint: 50 developers, fast solo competition, fully fed and watered. $50 in Cursor Credits for every selected participant, $1,200 prize pool (six $200 spots). Monday April 13 in Back Bay — register on Luma; approval required.
upcoming.longDescription:
We're thrilled to announce our inaugural Hack-a-Sprint, co-hosted with inkbox.ai and genesisfund.co. We're bringing together 50 developers for a fast-paced, solo competition.

Whether you're building something brand new or pushing the limits of your current project, this is your chance to code, compete, and connect with the Boston Cursor community. You will be fully fed and watered.

The Perks & Prizes

Guaranteed bounty: Every selected participant receives $50 in Cursor Credits.
Prize pool: $1,200 total, split across six $200 Cursor Credit winning spots.

When & Where

Date: Monday, April 13, 2026
Location: Back Bay, Boston — exact address is shown on Luma after your registration is approved.

The Schedule

4:00 PM — Meet, greet, and get set up.
4:30 PM – 7:00 PM — The Hack-a-Sprint.
7:00 PM – 8:00 PM — Scoring and judging. Results use participants, judges, and AI; a detailed rubric will be shared soon.

How to Secure Your Spot

We have exactly 50 spots; everyone competes individually. Selection happens about one week before the event. Priority order:

1. Open source: Most merged PRs to github.com/rogerSuperBuilderAlpha/cursor-boston — get to work!
2. Community profile: Completed profile on cursorboston.com.
3. Discord: Member of the Cursor Boston Discord server.
4. Sign-up order: Early birds get the worm.

Registration is subject to host approval. Luma may ask you to verify token ownership with your wallet as part of checkout.

We qualify participants rigorously. If selected people don't meet criteria or drop out, we pull aggressively from the waitlist.

Get Involved

Didn't make the top 50? Join the waitlist — drop-outs happen. Want to judge instead? Contact the hosts on Luma.

More info is coming soon; don't wait to request your spot.

agenda.title: Hack-a-Sprint

faq.question: Who should apply?
faq.answer: Developers and builders who want to compete individually in a fast-paced hackathon. You must have a completed profile on cursorboston.com and be in the Cursor Boston Discord server.

faq.question: Is this a team or solo competition?
faq.answer: Solo! Everyone competes individually. We have exactly 50 spots.

faq.question: Will food be provided?
faq.answer: Yes. You will be fully fed and watered.

faq.question: Is the date and venue final?
faq.answer: Date: Monday, April 13, 2026 in Back Bay, Boston. The exact address appears on Luma after your registration is approved.

faq.question: What if I don't make the top 50?
faq.answer: Join the waitlist! Drop-outs happen, and you could be next in line. You can also contact us to become a judge.

whatToBring: A project idea or problem you want to explore
A willingness to build with new tools

upcoming.title: Cursor Boston/AIC BTW Open Source Workshop
upcoming.subtitle: Hands-on open source contributions with Cursor as your AI co-pilot — co-hosted with AIC

venue.name: TBD (exact address after approval)
venue.address: Register on Luma to see the venue address after you're approved.

upcoming.description: Open Source Drop-In Workshop co-hosted by Cursor Boston and AIC. A hands-on half-day session with two tracks: contribute to the Cursor Boston repo (beginner-friendly) or tackle curated impactful open source projects using Cursor as your AI co-pilot. Walk away with merged PRs, Cursor credits, and practical open source skills. Saturday May 30 in Boston — register on Luma; approval required.
upcoming.longDescription:
We're thrilled to announce the Open Source Drop-In Workshop, co-hosted by Cursor Boston and AIC (AI Community).

This is a hands-on half-day session designed to teach you how to contribute to real open source projects using Cursor as your AI co-pilot. Whether you've never opened a pull request or you're a seasoned contributor looking for high-impact projects, there's a track for you.

Two Tracks

Track A — Cursor Boston Repo (Beginner-Friendly): Learn the fundamentals of open source contribution by working directly on the Cursor Boston community repository. Mentors will guide you through forking, branching, making changes, and opening your first PR.

Track B — Curated Impactful Projects: For more experienced developers, we've hand-picked a set of impactful open source projects with beginner-friendly issues. Use Cursor's AI capabilities to understand unfamiliar codebases quickly and ship meaningful contributions.

What You'll Walk Away With

Merged pull requests on real open source projects, Cursor credits, and practical skills you can use immediately. This isn't a lecture — it's a working session where you'll ship real code.

Hosted by Roger Hunt, Ananya Shah, Janna, Kanal Patel, and Jason Morris.
perks: Merged PRs on real open source projects

agenda.title: Doors Open & Setup
agenda.description: Arrive, get settled, install Cursor if needed, and meet fellow contributors

agenda.title: Welcome & Track Overview
agenda.description: Introduction to open source contribution, overview of Track A (Cursor Boston repo) and Track B (curated projects), and how to pick your track

agenda.title: Hands-On Contribution Session
agenda.description: Work on your chosen track with mentor support. Fork repos, pick issues, write code with Cursor as your AI co-pilot, and open pull requests

agenda.title: PR Review & Merging
agenda.description: Get your pull requests reviewed and merged. Celebrate your contributions with the group

agenda.title: Wrap-Up & Networking
agenda.description: Share what you built, exchange contacts, and connect with the Cursor Boston and AIC communities

faq.question: Do I need open source experience?
faq.answer: Not at all! Track A is specifically designed for beginners who have never contributed to open source. Mentors will walk you through every step, from forking a repo to opening your first pull request.

faq.question: What are the two tracks?
faq.answer: Track A focuses on contributing to the Cursor Boston community repository — perfect for beginners. Track B is for more experienced developers who want to contribute to curated, impactful open source projects. You can choose your track when you arrive.

faq.question: What do I get for participating?
faq.answer: You'll walk away with merged pull requests on real open source projects, Cursor credits, and practical skills for contributing to open source using AI-powered tools.

faq.question: Where is the venue?
faq.answer: The venue is TBD. The exact address will appear on Luma after your registration is approved.

faq.question: Will food be provided?
faq.answer: Details on food and refreshments will be announced closer to the event date. Check the Luma page for updates.

faq.question: Who is hosting this event?
faq.answer: This event is co-hosted by Cursor Boston and AIC (AI Community). Hosts include Roger Hunt, Ananya Shah, Janna, Kanal Patel, and Jason Morris.

whatToBring: A willingness to contribute to open source

past.title: Cafe Cursor Boston
past.subtitle: Boston's First Cursor Community Gathering
past.description: Join us for the first Cursor community event in Boston. Featuring co-working, Cursor workshops for entrepreneurs, engineers, and non-technical folks. Meet the community, learn something new, and grab some coffee.
past.longDescription:
Cafe Cursor Boston is an intimate gathering designed to bring together developers, entrepreneurs, and curious minds who want to explore the future of AI-powered development.

Whether you're a seasoned engineer looking to supercharge your workflow, an entrepreneur wanting to build faster, or someone who's never written a line of code but is curious about what's possible — this event is for you.

We'll have hands-on workshops, open co-working time, and plenty of opportunities to connect with fellow community members over great coffee.

agenda.title: Doors Open & Coffee
agenda.description: Grab some coffee, settle in, and meet fellow attendees

agenda.title: Welcome & Introductions
agenda.description: Brief overview of the day and community introductions

agenda.title: Workshop Sessions
agenda.description: Hands-on workshops tailored to different skill levels

agenda.title: Open Co-working
agenda.description: Work on your projects with community support

agenda.title: Networking & Close
agenda.description: Wrap up and connect with new friends

faq.question: Do I need coding experience?
faq.answer: Not at all! We have workshops designed specifically for non-technical folks. Cursor makes it possible to build without writing code yourself.

faq.question: What should I bring?
faq.answer: Bring your laptop with Cursor installed (we can help you set it up if needed). We'll provide coffee, snacks, and good vibes.

faq.question: Is there parking available?
faq.answer: Details about parking and public transit will be shared closer to the event date.

faq.question: Can I come for just part of the event?
faq.answer: Absolutely! Feel free to drop in and out as your schedule allows.

coworkingInfo.description: Reserve your spot at one of our 2-hour coworking sessions. Work on your projects alongside fellow developers with access to mentors and community support.
